articles.title: Linear Programming
articles.contents:
Concepts






Programming Application (R)
Problem Statement

A company produces two models of chairs: 4P and 3P. The model 4P needs 4
legs, 1 seat and 1 back. On the other hand, the model 3P needs 3 legs
and 1 seat. The company has a initial stock of 200 legs, 500 seats and
100 backs. If the company needs more legs, seats and backs, it can buy
standard wood blocks, whose cost is 80 euro per block. The company can
produce 10 seats, 20 legs and 2 backs from a standard wood block. The
cost of producing the model 4P is 30 euro/chair, meanwhile the cost of
the model 3P is 40 euro/chair. Finally, the company informs that the
minimum number of chairs to produce is 1000 units per month. Define a
linear programming model, which minimizes the total cost (the production
costs of the two chairs, plus the buying of new wood blocks).

Credit: “Modeling and Solving Linear Programming with R” by Jose M.
Sallan, Oriol Lordan and Vincenc Fernandez. Page 57, section 3.5 and R
bloggers.
\(x_{4p}\) is the number of 4P
chairs to be produced.
\(x_{3p}\) is the number of 3P
chairs to be produced.
\(x_w\) is the number of wood
blocks to be bought.
Now we can define \(\hat X =
\begin{pmatrix} x_{4p} \\ x_{3p} \\ x_w \end{pmatrix}\) as the
decision variable vector. Note that it must be \(\hat X \geq 0\).
We would like to minimize the total cost so we must set our objective
function as follows
\[cost(x_{4p}, x_{3p}, x_w) = 30 x_{4p} +
40 x_{3p} + 80 x_w = MIN(cost) \]
which means that \(\hat C = \begin{pmatrix}
30 \\ 40 \\ 80 \end{pmatrix}\).

articles.title: Yuhan Wu's Personal Website
articles.description: Welcome to the website. I hope you enjoy it!
articles.contents:
Introduction
Hello, my name is Yuhan Wu. I am currently a student at Newton North
High School. When I was young, I have always wondered what are the real
application of all the math and science knowledge that I’ve learned. I
discovered the answer to this question after my first math modeling
competition; I came to the realization and the fascination of the power
of data analytics and mathematical modeling. It is the pleasure of
creation and seeing its application that keeps motivate me and pushes me
further in the journey of math and data.
I am an amateur badminton player and a pianist (Performance
at Asian Night). On to food, I am a crazy burritos lover since my
first exposure to it during a math modeling competition.
This website will keep track of my several main projects throughout
high school, as well as progress and knowledge development as I dive
deeper into the fields of applied mathematics and data science.
Hope you can find this website interesting and can learn a little
more about me!
Welcome again! ฅ՞•ﻌ•՞ฅ
Yuhan
Modeling Exploration
This section record my explorations and the learning of different
algorithms, theories and methods of data analysis.
These topics includes:
Linear Programming
Graph Theory
Interpolation and
Fitting
Analytic Hierarchy Process
(AHP)
Integer Programming
Dynamic Programming
Differential Equation
Modeling
Game Theory
Queueing Theory
Inventory Theory
Projects Overview
